# About Security Codes

# About Security Codes

To protect your account and sensitive information, some Amazon services require additional
verification through a security code.

**If you haven’t received your code:**

1. Verify your mobile number is correct.
   - In Your Account, visit
     **Login and Security** to confirm your number is correct.
2. Request a new code
   - Select Resend Code.
3. Check that Amazon messages (35213) aren’t blocked
   - On iOS:
     - Go to Settings > Apps > Messaging
     - Ensure SMS is enabled.
     - Navigate to Blocked Contacts and make sure 35213 isn’t listed.
     - Unblock the number if needed, then request the code again.
   - On Android:
     - Go to Apps > Settings > Apps.
     - Tap the three vertical dots and select Special Access.
     - Note: On some devices, this may appear under App list > Filter > Show system apps.
     - Select Use Premium Text Message Services and set to Always allow.
     - If you still don’t receive codes, contact your carrier to enable Short Code SMS.
4. Try again
   - Request a new code and enter it when prompted.
path: Account Settings/About Security Codes.md/1

# Approve a Teen's Order

# Approve a Teen's Order

When a teen places an order, a parent can review and approve the order through Your Orders, by email, or by text.

**Note:** As of April 7, 2025, Amazon is no longer accepting new signups for the Teen program.
The information below applies only to existing Teen accounts.

If your teen's order is below your preset spending limit, you won’t be asked to approve
your teen’s order. You'll be notified of the purchase. You can change these settings
in the teen’s profile in Manage Your Household.

To approve a teen's order do one of the following:

- Go to Your Orders to see orders placed by your teen. In the Amazon app, Your Orders can be accessed through the main app menu. Approve or decline each order awaiting
  approval.
- When you receive a text from Amazon requesting approval, reply with "Y" to approve
  the order. This option is not available if there are multiple orders waiting for approval
  or if the order contains more than four items. Instead, the text message will direct
  you to Your Orders to approve the order.
- When you receive an email from Amazon requesting approval, select the approval link
  in the email to approve the order.

**Note:** After 48 hours, unapproved orders expire.

# Manage a 'Multiple accounts use the same email' Error

# Manage a 'Multiple accounts use the same email' Error

If you get an error saying 'Multiple accounts use the same email address' when trying
to sign in to Amazon, follow these steps to troubleshoot.

Amazon no longer supports using the same email address to create or sign in to more
than one account. If you receive this error, you must either sign in to the default
account for your email, which is the one you signed into most recently, or change
the email for your secondary account.

You cannot use the same email address to create or sign in to more than one Amazon
account. To fix the issue, sign in to your main email account, the one you used last,
or update the email address for your secondary account.

**You received this error message and you don't recall having multiple Amazon accounts:**

This error is not an indication that your account security is compromised. It can
happen if:

1. You unintentionally made another Amazon account before, possibly when you got locked
   out and had to create another one.
2. Someone else may now own your old email address and used it to create an Amazon account.
   They don't have access to your Amazon account.

**You do not intend to keep using this secondary account:**

Select the
Sign in to default account button. Sign in using the password for your default account. If you've forgotten
your password, you can
reset your password. To delete your secondary account, first change the email address, and then sign
in.

# Manage an ‘E-mail address already in use’ Error

# Manage an ‘E-mail address already in use’ Error

If you see an 'E-mail address already in use' error when trying to use an email address
on an Amazon account, follow these troubleshooting steps.

The same email address can't be used for multiple Amazon accounts. An ‘E-mail address
already in use’ error message will be displayed if you attempt to use the same email
address on more than one Amazon account.

**If the error occurs when trying to create a new Amazon account:**

1. Attempt to sign into the Amazon account with your password. If you have forgotten
   your password, you can reset your password.
2. If you're still unable to sign in and you're certain you've never created an Amazon
   account using this address, try creating an account using:
   - another email address you own.
   - an email alias. Check with your email provider if they support creating an email alias
     with your email account.
   - your mobile phone number.

**If the error occurs when trying to update the email address on your Amazon account:**

1. You may already own an existing account with that email address.
2. To use that email address on another account, you need to release it from your existing
   account. To release the email address:
   1. Sign in to your existing account that already uses the email address. If you don't
      remember the password, reset your password.
   2. Change the email address on your existing account to a different email address you
      own.
   3. Sign out of your existing account.
   4. Sign into the current account and update the email address, to the email address you
      released from the existing account.

# Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails

# Recover Your Account after Two-Step Verification Fails

If Two-Step Verification fails and you can't access your account, you can try to
recover your account.

To regain access to your account, you'll need to verify your identity by providing
a scan or photo of a government-issued identity document. To protect your account,
we're unable to make changes to your Two-Step Verification settings until your identity
is successfully verified. If you're able to sign in to your account, you'll have access
to make changes to your account.

Before you begin account recovery, try to sign in with a registered backup method
or from a trusted device.

If you still can't sign in, to recover your account:

1. Go to Two-Step Verification Account Recovery.
2. Follow the on-screen instructions to upload your identity document.

   Make sure that your name and address, and the issuing authority (for example, state
   or country) are visible.

   Cover, conceal, or remove sensitive information such as account numbers or identification
   numbers.

The verification process can take 1-2 days to complete. We'll email you once we disable
the Two-Step Verification and then you'll be able to sign in to your account with
your password. We may still require additional security steps during sign-in to protect
your account, such as providing a one-time code sent to your email or your primary
phone number added to your Amazon account.

# Removing a Teen Login from an Amazon Household

# Removing a Teen Login from an Amazon Household

Teen Logins are available to teens ages 13-17 years. Teens (18+) are eligible for
their own regular Amazon account.

Any time after a teen reaches the age of 18, the adult or teen may remove the Teen
Login from the Amazon Household. A regular Amazon account receives access to the following:

- An independent shopping experience without the need for adult oversight
- Freedom to use personal payment methods
- Ability to choose Prime memberships

To manually remove the Teen Login:

1. Go to Manage Your Household.
2. Choose **Edit Profile** of the Teen Login.
3. Select **Remove from Household**.
4. On the pop-over box, select Remove teen to confirm the removal.

A former Teen Login no longer has access to the payment method or Prime benefits of
the Amazon household (if applicable), but does retain:

- The Amazon Household shipping address
- Order history
- Saved items
- Wishlist-Preferences

Any active orders placed using the Teen Login that have been approved will still be
fulfilled. Orders currently awaiting approval will be cancelled and the payment method
will not be charged.

**Note:**

- Any items in the Shopping Cart as well as the Gift Card balance carry to the regular
  Amazon account.
- Teens currently enrolled in higher education studies have the option to register for
  Prime Student when they transition their account. To learn more about Prime Student
  benefits, visit Join Prime Student.
- Amazon Household allows sharing of Prime benefits (if applicable) for two unique adult
  accounts. If there is currently only one adult in your Amazon Household, visit Add an Adult to an Amazon Household.

# What Is a Teen Login?

# What Is a Teen Login?

A teen login allows teens (aged 13 to 17) to shop and share payment methods and Prime
benefits with adults in an Amazon Household.

**Note:** As of April 7, 2025, Amazon is no longer accepting new signups for the Teen program.
The information below applies only to existing Teen accounts, which can continue to
be used until the teen turns 18.

With a teen login, teens (aged 13 to 17) have their own Amazon login to shop or stream
content. Teens pay using a gift card, their credit card, or the shared payment method
of the adults in an Amazon Household.

Adults choose one of the following approval options:

- Automatic approval on all purchases.
- Automatic approval up to a specified amount.
- Manual approval of all orders.

For orders that require manual approval, adults receive a notification to review the
items, shipping, and payment information before the transaction is processed. Adults
can review the order by text or from Your Orders. Regardless of approval settings, adults receive itemized notifications for every
order and can cancel or return any order.

Adults who have a Prime Membership can share select benefits with their teen including:

- Prime Shipping Benefits
- Amazon Elements for Prime
- Early Access to deals
- Free Prime Video
- Unlimited full-resolution photo storage with Amazon Photos

# What is Two-Step Verification?

Two-Step Verification is a feature that adds an extra layer of security to your account
log-in.

When you try to log in, 2-Step Verification sends you a unique security code. When
you sign up for 2-Step Verification, you can choose to receive the security code by
text message or authenticator app.

You need to enter both the code and your password to log in.

To enable 2-step verification, go to

Your Account

1. In Your Account, select Login & security.
2. Follow the on-screen instructions.

## To disable 2-step verification:

# Why Can't I Sign into My Account?

# Why Can't I Sign into My Account?

If you can't log into Amazon, you may be entering incorrect account information or
you may need to reset your password.

If you can’t sign into Amazon, verify the following before resetting your password:

- **Email Address:** Your email address must be in the format username@domain.com (.net, .org). If you
  have multiple email addresses, make sure you're using the correct email address and
  password combination.
- **Mobile Phone Number**: If you created your account with a mobile phone number, you'll need to use this
  number to log in. Make sure to include your country code.

# How to identify fake emails

# How to identify fake emails

Learn to identify fake emails and protect your personal information.

If you received correspondence regarding an order you didn't place, it likely wasn't
from Amazon. Visit Your Orders to review your order history. To report a suspicious
email, visit

Report a Scam

Scammers imitate email addresses and communication styles to send what appears as
legitimate Amazon emails.

To check whether an email is from Amazon:

- **Check
  Message Center.**
  Message Center will have all email communication sent by Amazon. If the email doesn't appear in
  Message Center, then it wasn't sent by Amazon.
  Report a Scam now.
- **Look for misspellings or added/substituted characters in the sender address.**
  Approved Amazon emails contain 'Amazon.com' and are sent from an
  Amazon.com email address. Common email providers will show the Amazon smile logo icon in their
  inbox when Amazon is the actual sender.
  Visit
  BIMI
  to find out which email providers have enabled our brand logo to be displayed.
- **Typos, grammatical errors, or links to websites that resemble Amazon, but aren’t Amazon.** Legitimate Amazon websites have a dot '.' before 'Amazon.com' such as https://'something'.Amazon.com.
- Legitimate Amazon emails may include links to secure Amazon pages instead of attachments.
  Amazon may send attachments as links for security reasons. Always ensure the link
  directs to an Amazon domain (for example, https://www.amazon.com/...).

  information if the request is suspicious.

# Identifying a scam

# Identifying a scam

Learn to identify, prevent, and report scams.

You can visit the following Help pages for additional tips to identify a fake communication:

How to identify fake emails

How to identify fake text messages/SMS

How to identify fake phone calls

How to identify fake websites

## What is an impersonation scam?

Impersonation scams occur when a scammer pretends to be a trustworthy organization
or person in order to steal your money or personal information. Impersonation scams
are perpetrated in a wide variety of ways - by phone, email, text, or even by messaging
you on social media. Scammers aim to have you make a payment of some kind, share personal
information, or give them access to your computer. They do so by pretending to be
a well-known organization with whom you've likely engaged.

## How do I identify an impersonation scam?

Impersonation scams can be difficult to identify. Here are some red flags indicating
an impersonation scam:

1. **False urgency**. Scammers often try to create a sense of urgency to persuade you to do what they're
   asking.
2. **Asking for personal information**. Scammers may try to persuade you to provide personal information over the phone
   such as addresses, payment information, account login credentials, or financial information.
3. **Purchases outside of Amazon**. Scammers may try to ask you to send money outside of the Amazon website or mobile
   app through a third-party site or wire transfer.

# Report Suspicious Activity

# Report Suspicious Activity

Amazon is committed to providing a safe and trusted shopping experience. If you believe that any product, seller, or activity is suspicious, follow these steps
to report it. For order questions, go to Your Orders instead of submitting a report.

**Submit a report on the product page.**

1. Log into your Amazon account on your desktop, mobile or Amazon Shopping app,
2. Navigate to the product page,
3. Select "Report an issue with this product or seller", and
4. Select "This product or content is illegal, unsafe or suspicious"or "I have an issue
   with a Seller" to report suspicious activity,
5. Follow the prompts to enter details about your issue and submit.

**Contact Us.** Please visit our Customer Service page and select "Report Something Suspicious". You can find this option under "Help with
something else". You will be offered a choice between reporting a suspicious phone
call, email or SMS received or reporting a suspicious product or seller. If you would
like to report a suspicious product or seller, select that option too, then speak
with a Customer Service agent via phone or chat.

**Report a suspicious phone call, message, or other scam.** Follow the instructions in Report a Scam to report suspicious emails, phone calls, text messages, unsolicited packages, or
gift card scams.

**Stolen goods.** The sale of stolen property is illegal and strictly prohibited in Amazon. If you
suspect that a product for sale on Amazon.com is stolen, contact Customer Service.

# Scam trends

# Scam trends

Review a sample of common scam trends. Scammers are creative and shift their tactics
constantly. This list is NOT comprehensive of all scammer tactics.

If you think you're being scammed, visit
Identifying a scam for more information on identifying, preventing, and reporting scams.

We've partnered with the Better Business Bureau to provide customers with a
Scam Tracker, a searchable database of common scams. The Scam Tracker enables you to search for
suspicious communications reported by others by email, URL, phone number, and more.

## Order confirmation scams

Order confirmation scams involve unexpected communications that often refer to an
unauthorized purchase. Scammers ask you to act urgently to confirm or cancel the purchase.
They may try to convince you to provide payment details, install software, or purchase
gift cards.

If you received unexpected correspondence regarding an order, verify your orders by
logging into your Amazon account. Only legitimate purchases will appear in your
order history. You can also contact Customer Service for support.

## Tech support scams

Scammers create fake websites claiming to provide tech support for your Amazon devices
and services. Customers who land on these pages are lured to contact the scammer and
fall prey to their schemes.

Go directly to the
help section of our website when seeking help with Amazon devices or services. If you use a search
engine, use caution. Legitimate Amazon websites contain 'Amazon.com' such as 'Amazon.com/support.'

## Prime Membership scams

Membership fee scams claim that a membership fee is owed, or that there's an issue
with your membership. Scammers try to convince you to provide payment or bank account
information to reinstate a membership. Amazon will never ask you to provide payment
information for products or services over the phone. Visit the
Message Center on the Amazon website or mobile app to review authentic emails from Amazon. To verify
your
Prime Membership or make payments, log in to Amazon, and under
**Your Account** select
**Prime Membership**.

## Account suspension/deletion scams

Account suspension/deletion scams claim your account will be suspended or deleted
unless you take specific action. Actions may include selecting a fraudulent link or
providing information like payment details or login credentials, to 'verify your account'.
Amazon never asks for your password or for sensitive personal information over the
phone or on any external website. Don’t access any links or provide your information
to anyone over the phone without authenticating the email or phone call. If you have
questions about your account status, visit the Amazon website or mobile app to view
your account details. Authentic emails from Amazon are logged in the
Message Center.

## Fake recall/refund scams

Fake recall or refund scams involve fraudulent messages claiming your recent purchase
has safety issues and needs immediate action. Scammers promise refunds but direct
you to fake websites or unofficial channels to steal your personal information.

Be wary of false urgency scammers that pressure you to act immediately. Amazon will
never request sensitive information outside of Amazon's website or app. We do not
send text messages about recalls. While our recall email notifications may include
links to Amazon pages, be cautious of suspicious communications. Don't click links
in suspicious texts or emails, and don't respond to them. Take time to verify any
urgent requests through Amazon channels.

To verify a recall or safety alert, log into your Amazon account and check the
Your Recalls and Product Safety Alerts page. Authentic Amazon emails can be found in the
Message Center.

To report suspicious communications, visit
Report a scam.

## Recruitment scams

Recruitment scams are offering fictitious job opportunities, and usually come through
unofficial channels, websites, emails, and text messages. They offer unsolicited job
opportunities that claim to be from Amazon. They usually request that you provide
personal information and payments to apply for jobs that don't exist.

It's important to understand that these fraudsters are Amazon impersonators. Legitimate
Amazon job opportunities never require upfront payment or sensitive personal information
during the application process. All official Amazon job postings can be found at
Amazon.jobs.

## International Return Methods

Amazon offers multiple return methods for internationally fulfilled items.

## UPS Drop-Off Returns

Customers shipping from Amazon.com to Canada may receive:
- Prepaid UPS return labels
- Access to UPS drop-off locations

## DHL Express Drop-Off

Available in select countries.

Customers can:
- Drop packages at DHL locations

## DHL Express Pickup

Customers selecting DHL pickup:
- Receive confirmation emails with instructions
- Can schedule pickups through MyDHL+

Pickup scheduling includes:
- Preferred location
- Preferred date
- Preferred time

## Self-Paid Return Labels

Customers using their own shipping labels may receive:
- Reimbursement up to $25 for return postage

Eligibility depends on:
- Country
- Receipt verification

## Heavy and Bulky Item Returns

Heavy or bulky items include products:
- Weighing 50 lbs or more
- Longer than 59 inches
- With girth exceeding 130 inches

Girth formula:
- length + 2 × (width + height)

These returns may:
- Require specialty carriers
- Require scheduled home pickup
- Include additional return shipping fees

## Discounted Purchase Returns

Returning items that qualified for discounts may reduce refund amounts.

## Gift Returns

Gift recipients can initiate returns using:
- 17-digit order numbers
- Packing slips
- Digital gift receipts

If exchanges are needed:
- Customers must return the gift
- Place a new order separately

Kindle books received as gifts may qualify for Amazon Gift Card exchanges before acceptance.

## Bundle Returns

Items purchased in Bundle with Savings promotions:
- Must be returned together
- Do not qualify for partial bundle refunds

# Account Closure and Data Deletion

## Closing an Amazon Account

Customers can permanently close their Amazon account and request deletion of personal information.

## Steps to Close an Account

To submit an account closure request:
1. Go to Close Your Amazon Account
2. Sign into the account you want to close
3. Review products and services associated with the account
4. Select a reason for closure from the dropdown menu
5. Select the checkbox confirming permanent closure and data deletion
6. Select “Close my Account”

## Verification Requirements

Amazon sends a confirmation notification through:
- Email
- Text message

Customers must respond within 5 days to verify the closure request.

## Multiple Accounts

Customers with multiple Amazon accounts:
- Must submit separate closure requests for each account

This ensures Amazon has proper authorization for every account.

## Consequences of Closing an Account

After closure:
- The account becomes permanently inaccessible
- Closed accounts cannot be restored
- Customers must create a new account for future purchases

## Global Account Closure Effects

Closing an Amazon account affects:
- amazon.com
- amazon.fr
- amazon.com.mx
- Other Amazon global sites using the same credentials

## Impact on Other Amazon Services

After closure, customers lose access to:
- Seller accounts
- Brand Registry accounts
- Advertising accounts
- Other Amazon business services

## Reopening Access

Closed accounts cannot be reopened.

To use Amazon again:
- Customers must create a completely new account

# About Customs Regulations for Identity Documents

# About Customs Regulations for Identity Documents

When you place an order for a product from Amazon.com, Identity Documents (ID information)
may be required for customs clearance.

Customs regulations in certain countries require you to provide ID for customs clearance.
Amazon safely & securely stores the ID information in order to transmit the information
to the carrier and Customs officials. This process allows your packages to clear Customs
without additional delay.

When you provide your ID information for an address, the ID information links to that
delivery address. We use the information provided for that shipment as well as future
shipments to that address. The name on the shipping address must match the name on
the provided ID information. If you're shipping to someone other than yourself, add
the ID information of the recipient. To edit the ID information linked to a delivery
address, go to Your Addresses.

To delete the ID information from your Amazon account, go to Manage saved IDs. We immediately remove the ID information from your saved IDs page and all addresses
in your address book. Permanent deletion from the Amazon database can take up to 30
days. If you don't use the ID information over a 90-day period, we automatically delete
the ID from Your Account and Amazon systems.

Errors or missing information may cause delays beyond the original delivery date.
The carrier may contact you for further information to complete those orders.

**Note:**

- Customs offices in some countries and regions require the importer of record (IOR)
  to provide a particular form of identification (ID) before releasing a shipment. You
  may be required to provide an ID number (such as national ID), photograph of ID, or
  other document.

# Amazon Map Tracking

# Amazon Map Tracking

You can follow the progress of selected shipped packages in real time on a map.

We'll notify you if you can track your package. To access Amazon Map Tracking, select
Track Package from Your Orders or your shipment confirmation email. On the day of delivery, you can see the map when the driver is
getting close to you.

If you sign up for delivery alerts on our app, we'll inform you when the map is available
to view.

Map tracking allows you to arrange to be at the property when your parcel is due for delivery, or to nominate a safe place for your delivery.

Sometimes, a driver may need to change their route. You'll continue to see the map,
but won't see the number of stops. You can still expect your parcel that day.

You must be signed into your Amazon.com account to view the map.

If we're delivering an order to a confidential address, such as a wish list or gift
address, we'll disable map tracking to protect the privacy of the recipient.

**Note:** Some carriers don't use Map Tracking.

# Availability Alerts

# Availability Alerts

If we don't have an official release date for an item, we may not be able to make
it available for pre-order. However, in many cases, you can sign up to be notified
when the item is available for purchase.

If we can offer this service for the item that interests you, an Alert Me box appears on the right side of the product information page. To use this service,
enter your email address in the space provided and select Sign up.

**Note:** Signing up for notification doesn't reserve the item for you. When you receive our
email saying that the item is available, return to our website to place your order
before the stock is gone again.

Prices on Amazon.com change frequently. The price in the email you receive is the best offer available
when the email was sent, but that price may have changed by the time you return to
the website to place an order.

# Billing and Shipping Addresses

# Billing and Shipping Addresses

To avoid delays, provide error free billing and shipping addresses.

Here are a few tips to keep in mind:

- Your billing address must match the address on your monthly credit card or bank statement.
- Leave fields blank if they don't apply to your address, for example, don't enter
  "N/A," "Not applicable," and so on.
- Add information in the correct fields, for example, don't enter city and state information in the Address Line 2 field. Use the city and state fields instead.
- Avoid using accent marks or non-English characters. Our system can't process these
  characters.
- Don't enter shipping or delivery instructions in the address fields. Amazon, our merchants,
  and marketplace sellers use a variety of carriers. Therefore, we can't assist with
  requests for specific shippers.

## Add Business Hours to your Addresses

You can add hours of operation when you are shipping your orders to a business facility:

- Go to **Your Account**
- Select Your Addresses
- Go to the address you want to edit and select Add delivery instructions.
- Make sure your Property type is set to Business.
- Add the hours of operations for the business. You can set separate scheduling options
  for weekends, weekdays or holidays. You can also add information like access codes
  or additional delivery instructions.
- Save changes

# Fulfilled by Amazon

# Fulfilled by Amazon

Items "Fulfilled by Amazon" (FBA) are offered by a third-party seller, but packed
and shipped to you from an Amazon Fulfilment Center.

All Amazon standard shipping rates and policies apply to these items when shipped
domestically, including free shipping on qualifying orders and premium shipping.

When we ship your order, we indicate the carrier company, shipping date and estimated
delivery date on the **Your Account** page. You can track your package in Your Orders.

Sometimes we will send you the exact product you ordered from the inventory of another
seller when it is closer to you (with both sellers' consent, of course). When we do
this, we'll notify you in your shipping confirmation email and you can view information
about both sellers in your order Invoice, which you can find on Your Orders.

We also handle all customer service and product returns for "Fulfilled by Amazon"
items.

# Packaging Programs

# Packaging Programs

About packaging and packaging programs for Amazon.com.

**The Climate Pledge**

On September 19, 2019, Amazon and Global Optimism announced The Climate Pledge -
a commitment to reach the Paris Agreement 10 years early. We are innovating and investing
to be net zero carbon by 2040 and run on 100% renewable energy by 2030. Since 2008,
sustainable packaging initiatives including Frustration-Free Packaging programs have
eliminated thousands of tons of packaging materials and over 1 billion boxes by promoting
easy-to-open, recyclable packaging and shipping products in their own packages without
additional shipping boxes.

We listen to our customers to identify new ways to reduce waste, improve manufacturer
packaging on items we sell, and innovate on the packaging we use in our fulfillment
centers. We gather packaging insights via customer service, online returns, and social
media to help us deliver the best possible experience while minimizing waste.

Learn more about our packaging programs at https://sustainability.aboutamazon.com/environment/packaging. To learn how to minimize your impact on the environment through reuse, refurbishing,
and recycling, visit Amazon Second Chance, which provides information on how to recycle Amazon packaging, devices and products,
along with information on how to find open-box and refurbished devices.

**Concerned about a Damaged Delivery or other issues with packaging?**

While our mission is to reduce the amount of packaging required while ensuring products
arrive to our customers undamaged, we recognize we don't always get it right.

# Replace a Missing Packing Slip

# Replace a Missing Packing Slip

If a packing slip is missing, you can print an invoice for your package.

To print an invoice:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. In the drop-down menu, choose the appropriate date range.
3. Scroll to find the order, and select View Invoice.

# Secure Delivery with a One-Time Password

# Secure Delivery with a One-Time Password

Due to the value of some items, a One-Time Password (OTP) is required on delivery
for some orders.

An OTP adds an extra layer of security to your packages.

If an OTP is required, we'll send a six-digit numeric OTP to your registered email
address, SMS or WhatsApp on the package delivery day.

You can also find the OTP:

- By selecting Track Package beside the order in Your Orders on the delivery day.
- By checking your registered email, SMS, or WhatsApp messages.

Your OTP is valid until the end of the delivery day.

Read the OTP that you’ve received to the driver in person and not over phone or intercom
to receive the package.

**Note:**

- If you're unable to receive the package, share the OTP  with someone you choose, and they can receive the package for you in person. We don't
  recommend sharing the OTP  with an unknown person.
- If no one can receive the package, we'll reattempt the delivery on the next working
  day.
- Don't add your phone number in the delivery address instructions.
- If you're not available to accept the delivery, ask the driver to reattempt the delivery
  when you or someone you choose will be at the delivery address.
- If you're not the package recipient or have ordered the item as a gift for someone
  else, forward the OTP to the recipient. Refer them to these guidelines.

# Shipping to Prisons

# Shipping to Prisons

Amazon.com makes deliveries to prisons. However, we recommend that you contact the prison first
to confirm that they accept deliveries and to ask about any special policies they
have.

The label on the outside of the package should only state the destination address,
Amazon.com company name, and return address. We can't include any special labels or instructions
on the package. You can't send gifts anonymously to inmates; the collection address
appears on the order invoice.

Because inmates can't sign for packages, they can only receive packages from US Mail. While Amazon.com often sends packages through US Mail, we also use UPS and FedEx. There's no way for you to pick a specific shipper. To avoid delivery problems, consider
having the package shipped to you and then shipping it to the prison using US Mail.

# Ships with other Items in the Cart

# Ships with other Items in the Cart

To reduce the number of boxes or deliveries to your address, items can be shipped
alongside other items in your cart or they can be shipped with items from a recently
placed order.

Items that show as "Ships in fewer boxes or deliveries" are often shipped with other
items in your cart leading to fewer boxes or deliveries to your address and thus,
reducing the packaging waste.

Items that show as "Ships with your recent order" are often shipped with other items
in your recently placed orders reducing the number of deliveries to your address.

Items that show as "Ships with other items in the cart" are often shipped with other
items in the cart at the time of purchase reducing both the number of boxes used and
the deliveries to your address.

These labels on products indicate that the product is typically delivered with at
least one other item recently ordered or in your cart, reducing the number of boxes
or deliveries to your address compared to items that cannot be delivered together.

An adult shopping profile is automatically created when an account is opened. You can create

# Bereavement support

# Bereavement support

We're sorry for your loss. Our dedicated support team is here to help.

## Protecting account information

To ensure privacy and security, we can only share account details or take most actions
related to an account when the request comes from the authorized estate manager.

If you can't provide all the required documents for authorization, we can still assist
with:

- Terminating subscriptions
- Stopping recurring deliveries
- Deactivating the account

For support regarding these topics, please email
bereavement-support-cs@amazon.com. Be sure to include the death certificate and the email or phone number associated
with the account.

## You have access to the email address or phone number associated with the account

If you have access to the email address or phone number associated with the account,
you can:

1. Sign in to the account to manage or close it.
2. Reset the password using the Forgot password link.

   Further guidance can be found on our
   Reset your password Help page.

## You don't have access to the account

To request account information, close an account, or make changes without having
access, verification is required.
This verification ensures that we only act on requests from an authorized representative.

To verify your authority, we require the following:

- **A copy of the death certificate,**
- **Proof of authorization** showing you're the legal representative of the deceased's estate (such as a probate
  or executor documentation),
- **A valid photographic ID** (such as a passport, driver's license, or ID card) of the authorized person, and
- **The email address/phone number** linked to the Amazon account.

# Cancel Items and Orders

# Cancel Items and Orders

You can cancel physical items or orders that haven't entered the shipping process
yet. This page does not include information about canceling Amazon Prime and other digital
subscriptions.

**About Cancelling an Item**

- You can cancel items Fulfilled by Amazon or shipped and sold by Amazon that haven't
  entered the shipping process.
- Orders sold and shipped by a third-party seller can typically be canceled within one
  business day.
- Once an order is in fulfillment, sellers are required to approve cancellation.
- To cancel an order that has not entered the shipping process, follow these steps:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Find the order that you want to cancel and select View or edit order .
3. Select Cancel items
4. Check box of the item that you want to cancel from the order. To cancel the entire
   order, select all the items.
5. Choose a reason for the cancellation and select Request cancellation.

**Cancellation Confirmation**

After submitting the cancellation, we'll send a confirmation message to the email
address on your account. For a history of your cancelled orders, visit Your Orders under Canceled Orders.

**Orders That Can't be Canceled**

- If your order is shipped directly from Amazon and can't be changed, refuse or return
  the package using our Online Returns Center.
- If your order is shipped directly from a third-party seller and can't be changed,
  contact the seller. For more information on contacting the seller, go to Contact Third-Party Sellers.

# Manage Your Amazon Accounts on Mobile Devices

You can add, remove, or switch between Amazon accounts using the Amazon shopping
app on your mobile device.

**Note:** Do not add your Amazon accounts to a device that is public, or shared with unknown
people, because anyone with access to the device will also have access to your Amazon
accounts. Any new Amazon accounts added to your mobile app inherit the app permissions,
such as camera roll access, or preferences, such as language, automatically. Updating
app settings on one Amazon account updates them for all.

To manage your Amazon account on a mobile device:

2. Go to Settings and select Switch Accounts.

When signing out on your device, you'll be signed out only on this device.

If you need to sign out from a device you no longer have access to, sign into your

# Power of Attorney (POA) Support

# Power of Attorney (POA) Support

To protect customer data, we can only share account information or handle an account
when the requester is the authorized person.

If you have access to the email address associated with the Amazon account, you can
sign in to proceed with your request. If you don't have access to the account, verification
is required to ensure that you are authorized to act on behalf of the account owner.

We will ask you to provide certain documentation:

- Official certified documentation showing that you are the authorized person to access
  the account;
- A valid photographic ID for the named authorized person listed on the letter of authorization
  such as a passport or driver’s license, and
- the email address associated with the Amazon account.

If you don't know the email address associated with the account, you can provide information
to help us authenticate the correct account. This information could be, for example,
a mobile number, unique charge ID, or order number from the account.

This documentation is required to ensure that:

- We act on the correct account only.
- We provide account information only to authorized persons with a legal entitlement
  to the information requested.

We assure you that the documents provided will be deleted after validation.

To continue with your request, attach the listed documentation and email it to our
dedicated support team:
poa-support@amazon.com.

# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

# Request the Closure of Your Account and the Deletion of Your Personal Information

You can submit a request for us to close your Amazon account and delete your personal
information permanently.

## Submit A Request To Close Your Amazon Account And Delete Your Personal Information:

Follow these steps to submit a request to close your Amazon account and delete your
personal information:

1. Go to
   Close Your Amazon Account.
2. Sign into the account you want to close.
3. Review the products and services associated with your account.
4. If you still wish to proceed, you can select a reason in the drop-down menu. Then
   select the box next to
   Yes, I want to permanently close my Amazon account and delete my data. To complete the closure, select
   Close my Account.
5. A confirmation notification will be sent to the email address associated with your
   account or via text message. You’ll need to reply within five days to verify your
   request.

## Consequences of Closing Your Account

- Once your account has been closed, all products and services accessed through your
  account will no longer be available to you, across any Amazon site globally. For example,
  submitting your account closure request through this website will also close your
  account on
  amazon.com,
  amazon.fr,
  amazon.com.mx, and all other global sites that use the same credentials to access services and
  products offered through those sites.
- Once your Amazon account is closed, you will not be able to access and manage any
  of your business accounts – such as Seller accounts, Brand Registry accounts, or Advertising
  accounts – as well as other products and services included in the closed accounts.

# Use Login with Amazon

# Use Login with Amazon

Login with Amazon allows you to easily and securely use your Amazon account to sign
into and share selected data, based on your consent, with third-party websites or
apps.

How to Use Login With Amazon

1. Go to a website or app that offers Login with Amazon.
2. Select Login with Amazon. 

   If our system doesn't detect an authenticated Amazon account, the Amazon-hosted sign-in
   screen will appear.
3. Sign in with your preferred method.
4. You'll be directed back to the website or app as a registered user, or prompted to
   complete registration.

Security:

Third parties are companies or developers who aren't Amazon. Only share your data
with third parties you trust.

When you use Login with Amazon, you'll always get an Amazon sign-in page with an Amazon
web address. You won't have to give your Amazon password directly to any other website
or app.

Managing Third-Party Access:

The first time you sign in, a screen requesting your consent to allow the third-party
application specific access to your data will appear. If you'd like to allow this
access, select Allow.

You can remove third-party access at any time by visiting Manage Login with Amazon.

Still Need Help? Try:

- Reset Your Password
- Change Your Account Settings
- Track Your Package

Mobile phone number accounts are Amazon accounts linked to a mobile phone number
instead of an email address.

3. Add your information and verify mobile phone number

You must then use your mobile phone number to sign in on all platform types, like
desktop and mobile browser.

To add an email address and get notifications like order updates via email:

If you don't have an email address linked to your Amazon account, you'll get notifications
and emails through the message center.

We also recommend to:

- Download the free Amazon shopping app to get real-time notifications about your order
  updates and when items go on sale.
- Enable push notifications, on your smartphone and tablet settings.

# What Happens When I Close My Account?

Once your account is closed, it's no longer accessible. You won't be able to access
your order history or print a proof of purchase or an invoice. The closure also affects
related customer accounts, features, and services that are linked to your email address
and password across any Amazon sites globally.

## Recommendations before closing your account

We recommend you download any of the content you uploaded to one of our services
(such as Amazon Photos), before closing your account.

We also recommend downloading any invoices you might need. Without access to your
order history and invoices, you'll likely be unable to provide proof of purchase.
Not having your invoices could impact your ability to make a warranty claim or seek
product support from the seller or manufacturer.

## Consequences of closing your account

Once your account has been closed, all products and services accessed through your
account will no longer be available to you, across any Amazon site globally. For example,
submitting your account closure request through this website will also close your
account on , amazon.fr, amazon.com.mx, and all other global sites that use the same credentials to access services and
products offered through those sites.

# Avoiding Payment Scams

# Avoiding Payment Scams

Protect yourself from fraud on the internet by identifying and avoiding internet
scams and phishing attempts.

When in doubt, ask the intended recipient for more information about the purpose and
safety of the requested payment. Don't send the payment until you're comfortable with
the transaction.

To avoid payment scams:

- Don't do business with a seller who directs you off the Amazon website. A legitimate
  Amazon seller transaction will never occur off the Amazon website.
- Don't send money (by cash, wire transfer, Western Union, PayPal, MoneyGram, or other
  means, including by Amazon Payments) to a seller who claims that Amazon or Amazon Payments will guarantee the transaction, refund your funds if you're not satisfied with the
  purchase, or hold your funds in escrow.
- Don't make a payment to claim lottery or prize winnings, or on a promise of receiving
  a large amount of money.
- Don't make a payment because you're guaranteed a credit card or loan.
- Don't respond to an internet or phone offer that you're not sure is honest.
- Don't make a payment to someone you don't know or whose identity you can't verify.
- Don't respond to emails that ask you to provide account information, such as your
  email address and password combination. Amazon will never ask you for personal information.
  To learn more, see Identifying Whether an Email is from Amazon.

For more information on Amazon.com Gift Card scams, see Common Gift Card Scams.

# Resolve a Declined Payment

# Resolve a Declined Payment

To protect your security and privacy, your bank can't provide Amazon with information
about why your payment was declined. Contact your bank directly to solve these payment
issues.

To retry a declined payment, go to .

Your Orders

To determine why your payment was declined, consider the following, and, if necessary,
contact your bank for more information:

- Have you exceeded your credit limit?
- Did you enter your credit card number, credit card expiration date, billing address,
  and phone number correctly in Your Account?
- Is your purchase outside of your normal spending range? Some banks will block transactions
  due to security concerns.
- Does your issuing bank have special policies regarding electronic or internet purchases?

To retry a declined payment:

1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Do one of the following:
   - Try again with a different payment method, as follows:
     1. Select Change Payment Method next to the order you want to modify.
     2. Select another payment method from your account or submit a new card number and select
        Confirm.
     3. Select Retry Payment Method next to the order.
   - Retry with your current payment method by selecting Retry Payment Method next to the order.

## **The unknown charge is a digital service payment**

If you purchased a digital service, such as a Prime Video channel subscription or
Kindle Unlimited, you are charged monthly. View your digital service charges at Your Digital Orders.

## **The unknown charge is a bank authorization**

Amazon contacts your bank to confirm your payment method when you place an order.
This authorization appears on your statement but is not an actual charge. Learn more
at Authorizations.

## **Pending or processing charges for cancelled orders**

If you cancelled an order before it shipped, an authorization might be visible on
your statement but is not an actual charge. Learn more at Authorizations.

## **The unknown charge is an Amazon Pay transaction**

Amazon Pay orders begin with 'P01' and are 14 digits long. Check your Amazon Pay Account for your order history. Go to the Amazon Pay Help pages for assistance.

## **An order was split into multiple shipments or sent to different addresses**

Amazon charges your payment method when each part of the order is shipped. This is
why you have separate charges on your statement.

Go to Your Transactions to match the charge amounts and dates on your statement with the associated order
number.

# About Currency of Preference on International Shopping

# About Currency of Preference on International Shopping

Currency of Preference on International Shopping allows you to shop in over 80 currencies.

Please note: Currency of Preference and currency settings are not available for domestic
US shoppers.

Amazon.com allows you to shop in over 80 currencies. Currency of Preference is a display-only
feature and allows you to see prices displayed in your preferred currency. You can
update your Currency of Preference by changing from USD (default currency) to your
preferred currency in the currency settings page. The available currencies for shopping
are listed below. Once you are ready to purchase your item, at checkout, an 'Order
Total' will display the total price of the order in the preferred currency. For payment,
a 'Payment Total' will display the amount your payment method will be charged in.

If your order is eligible at checkout for Amazon Currency Converter, you can elect
to pay with an eligible card, and the Payment Total will display the price in your
card currency you elected to pay in. Please note that payment in a different currency
is only possible for orders eligible for Amazon Currency Converter.

The currency you select is for reference use only. Prices may be displayed in your
selected currency for your convenience, and you can have prices displayed in your
chosen currency, even for orders not eligible for Amazon Currency Converter. At checkout,
if you wish to pay using your chosen currency, the actual sales price will have additional
fees which will be calculated at checkout.

# Amazon Currency Converter Exchange Rates

# Amazon Currency Converter Exchange Rates

The exchange rate is used to determine the payment total in your local currency while
placing your order.

When Amazon Currency Converter is enabled, the exchange rate is used to convert the
order subtotal to your local currency. The exchange rate displayed at checkout may
not match the rates published online or in other financial databases. Those exchange
rates are generally inter-bank rates that are for wholesale amounts and aren't available
to retail consumers. Amazon consistently works to negotiate competitive foreign exchange
rates with our bank service provider. The exchange rates used for Amazon Currency
Converter are generally updated daily.

If you return an item, we use the same exchange rate to calculate your refund, including
any fees.

**Note:**

- When using Amazon Currency Converter, your bank may still charge you a fee. Please
  contact your bank regarding these fees.
- For Kindle ebooks, the applicable exchange rate displays on the detail page.

- For more information about switching between your local currency and the default website
  currency, visit Select Your Currency for Amazon Currency Converter.

- Foreign exchange rates are provided by third parties, including Refinitiv for certain
  currencies. © 2018 – 2021 Refinitiv. All rights reserved. Republication or redistribution
  of Refinitiv content, including by framing or similar means, is prohibited without
  the prior written consent of Refinitiv. Refinitiv and the Refinitiv logo are trademarks
  of Refinitiv and its affiliated companies.

# Amazon Currency Converter Requirements

# Amazon Currency Converter Requirements

Use of Amazon Currency Converter is subject to certain requirements.

To qualify to use Amazon Currency Converter:

- You must pay with a Visa, Mastercard, or American Express credit or debit card denominated in a supported currency. To view supported currencies by payment method, go to Amazon Currency Converter Supported Currencies.
- Your purchase can't include subscriptions or digital products, except for Kindle eBooks.
- The estimated shipping window of all items must be within 14 or 30 days of placing your order, depending on your local currency.

**Note:**

To resolve a declined payment on an Amazon Currency Converter order, you must use
an eligible payment method denominated in the same currency as the original purchase
to maintain Amazon Currency Converter. For more information, go to Resolve a Declined Payment.

# Select Your Card Currency for Amazon Currency Converter

# Select Your Card Currency for Amazon Currency Converter

If your order is eligible for Amazon Currency Converter, the option to pay in your
card currency will be displayed at checkout.

Amazon Currency Converter uses information, such as the country or region your card
was issued in, to determine your card currency. If you want to change your card currency, you can update it at the checkout:

**Desktop**

1. From the **Payment method** section at checkout, select **Change**.
2. From your list of payment methods, select your payment method of choice.
3. While on your payment method, select **Change card currency**.
4. Select **My card is in a different currency**.
5. Select the currency of your payment method from the list.
6. Click **Use this payment method** to continue.

**Mobile**

1. From the **Payment information** section at checkout, select **Payment method**.
2. From your list of payment methods, select your payment method of choice.
3. Select **Change card currency**.
4. Select **My card is in a different currency**.
5. Choose the currency of your payment method from the list.
6. Click **Continue**.

# Amazon Returns Policy Overview

## Easy Shopping and Simple Returns

Amazon is committed to making shopping seamless and worry-free. Customers may return eligible items when purchases do not meet expectations or needs change.

Most items sold on Amazon.com can be returned within 30 days of delivery for:
- Refund
- Replacement
- Exchange

Items must generally be:
- In original condition
- Unused
- Returned with original packaging and accessories

## Return Eligibility

Customers should review return eligibility:
- On the product detail page before purchase
- In Order History after placing an order

Eligible items may qualify for:
- Free returns
- Nearby drop-off locations
- Box-free returns
- Label-free returns

## Refund Eligibility

A refund is issued after:
1. Amazon or the third-party seller receives the returned item
2. The item is inspected
3. The return is approved

Return processing may take:
- Up to 30 days under normal conditions
- Longer in exceptional situations

If additional information is required, Amazon contacts the customer with further instructions.

## Additional Help Resources

Customers may also need:
- Product support
- Missing package support
- Refund assistance
- A-to-z Guarantee support for third-party seller purchases

# Return Windows and Eligibility Exceptions

## Standard Return Window

Most items sold on Amazon.com can be returned within 30 days of delivery.

## 7-Day Return Window

Applicable to:
- Kindle books accidentally purchased from the Kindle Store that have not been read
- Digital textbooks, workbooks, and educational content that have not been downloaded
- Songs or albums accidentally purchased using Alexa from the Digital Music Store

## 15-Day Return Window

Applicable to:
- Apple brand products sold in new condition
- Boost Infinite brand products sold in new condition
- Amazon Haul items priced above $3

Notes:
- AppleCare+ cancellations follow plan terms
- Amazon Haul items priced at $3 or less are non-returnable and non-refundable

## 90-Day Return Window

Applicable to:
- Select Amazon Renewed items in Acceptable, Good, or Excellent condition
- Most nonperishable baby products
- Amazon Birthday Registry purchases made by someone other than the registry owner
- Custom Gift List purchases
- Mattresses excluding crib mattresses

## 180-Day Return Window

Applicable to:
- Amazon Wedding Registry purchases made by someone other than the registry owner

## 365-Day Return Window

Applicable to:
- Amazon Renewed Premium products
- Amazon Baby Registry purchases made by someone other than the registry owner

- Products with shipping restrictions

Products labeled as “Final Sale” are:
- Non-returnable
- Non-refundable

- Certain discounted items

## Damaged or Defective Non-Returnable Items

If a non-returnable or Final Sale item arrives:

- Defective
- Materially different from the order

Customers should contact Customer Service for assistance.

# Initiating, Packaging, and Sending Returns

## Starting a Return

To initiate a return:
1. Go to Your Orders
2. Select “Return Items” beside the eligible item

## Return Drop-Off Options

Amazon supports:
- Over 8,000 return locations
- Free returns for eligible items
- Box-free returns
- Label-free returns

Most return locations are within a 5-mile radius of customer addresses.

## Packaging Requirements

Returned items should include:
- Original manufacturer packaging
- Tags
- Accessories
- Components
- Manuals
- Certificates of authenticity
- Inserts

Additionally:
- Hygiene seals and liners must remain intact
- Items should be unused or in original condition

## Return Deadlines

Items must be returned before the “return by date” shown in:
- Return confirmation emails
- Return Request section in Order History

## Important Return Rules

Customers should:
- Follow return instructions for each item
- Avoid combining items from separate orders in the same package

## Personal Information Removal

Before returning electronics or devices containing personal information:
- Completely erase stored data
- Follow manufacturer instructions

Examples include:
- Computers
- Phones
- Electronics

## Refund Reversal Conditions

If a refund is issued before the item is returned:
- Customers may be charged if the item is not sent back

Charges are reversed once Amazon processes the returned item.

## Hazardous Item Returns

Defective hazardous items must be returned through instructions provided in the Online Returns Center.

# Return Fees, Damage Fees, and Partial Refunds

## Return Shipping Fees

Most items include at least one free return option.

Fees may apply when:
- Selecting paid drop-off or shipping methods
- Returning heavy or bulky items
- Having unusually high return rates

## Late Return Fees

Customers may incur fees when returns are not completed before the return deadline.

Fee structure:
- 20% of the item price during the first 30 days after the return-by date
- 100% of the item price afterward

## Damage Fees

Damage fees may apply if returned items:
- Are damaged
- Are missing parts
- Are not in original condition
- Have removed tags
- Show obvious signs of use

Fee structure:
- Up to 50% of the item price
- Amazon Luxury items may incur 100% damage fees

## Restocking Fees

A 100% restocking fee may apply to:
- Opened software
- Activated video games
- Opened collectible cards
- Board games
- Collectible figurines missing parts or packaging

## Partial Refund Policies

All returned items are inspected before refunds are issued.

Refunds may be reduced for:
- Customer use
- Damage
- Missing accessories
- Missing manuals
- Missing parts

## Currency-Converted Orders

For currency-converted orders:

# Refund Processing, Timelines, and Refund Types

## Refund Processing

Refunds are processed after Amazon receives and approves the returned item.

Customers receive email notifications with refund summaries after refunds are issued.

## Refund Timelines by Payment Method

| Refund Method | Estimated Timeline |
|---|---|
| Amazon Gift Card | 2–3 hours |
| Credit Card | 3–5 business days |
| Shop with Reward Points | Up to 5 business days |
| Debit Card | Up to 10 business days |
| Checking Account | Up to 10 business days |
| EBT Card | Up to 10 business days |
| Cash at Participating Locations | Up to 10 business days |
| Prepaid Credit Card | Up to 30 days |
| Promotional Certificate | No refund issued |

## Types of Refunds

### Advanced Refunds

Advanced refunds are issued when the carrier first scans the return instead of waiting for Amazon to process the item.

### Declined Refunds

If the original payment method cannot receive funds:
- Refunds are issued to Amazon account balance

Example:
- Expired credit cards

### Partial Refunds

Refund amounts may be reduced when items are:
- Used
- Damaged
- Missing parts

### Gift Refunds

Gift return refunds are issued to the person who initiated the return.

# About US State Sales and Use Taxes

# About US State Sales and Use Taxes

Items sold on Amazon Marketplaces and shipped to locations both inside and outside
the US, including territories, may be subject to tax.

## **How Tax is calculated**

The amount of tax charged on your order depends on many factors including the following:

- The identity of the seller
- The type of item or service purchased
- The time and location of fulfillment
- The shipment or delivery address of your order

These factors can change between the time you place an order and when your shipment
is complete. As a result, the tax calculated on your order may change. We display
an "Estimated Tax" to be collected at Check Out when confirming an order. The amounts
displayed as estimated tax may then be updated later when your order is finalized
and completed.

The tax rate applied to your order will be the combined state and local rates of
the address where your order is delivered to or fulfilled from. For example, if you
live in a state that does not impose a sales tax, you may still see tax calculated
on your order if shipped to another state. Moreover, the tax rates applied to your
order may also be different for a variety of reasons, such as a shipment to a residential
home versus a business address. Also, the total selling price of an item will generally
include item-level shipping and handling charges, item level discounts, and gift wrapping
charges. If applied at an order level, these charges, may be allocated to the individual
items in an order.

If you are ordering from Amazon.com and shipping to a country outside of the US,
you may be charged the applicable country tax rate.

**Note:**

- No tax is charged when purchasing gift cards; however, purchases paid for with gift
  cards may be subject to tax.

# About Hotel Deliveries



Shipping items to a hotel may result in complications not covered by Amazon.



If you choose to ship an item to a hotel, the following policy applies:



Provide complete address details, check delivery times and communicate with the hotel.



##### Before placing your order:



* Contact the hotel. Ask them about their policies for accepting packages. Confirm whether they need any specific information to ensure that you successfully get your item. Ask about any related fees and how early you can send the item.
* Add details to your address. You can add a new address to your Address Book. You can also add or modify instructions to existing addresses by selecting Add Delivery Instructions or Edit on the desired address. Include the following details:

  * Recipient’s full name (name on the reservation)
  * Name of the hotel
* Check your delivery times. Make sure that your delivery date during checkout matches when you are at the location.



##### After ordering:



* Inform the hotel. Let the front desk know that you’re expecting a package.
* Track your package. You can find tracking numbers and delivery updates in Your Orders. Visit the Tracking Your Package Help page for more details.



Amazon cannot issue refunds or replacements for packages that are delivered to a hotel but not received by the customer.



A delivery is considered successful once the package receives a delivery scan at the hotel’s front desk, concierge desk, or designated receiving area.

# About Items Shipped to Companies Not Affiliated with Amazon



Shipping items to a third-party company that isn’t affiliated with Amazon may result in complications not covered by Amazon.



Third-party companies that are not affiliated with Amazon include freight forwards, Buyer’s Clubs, third-party warehouses, and re-sellers. Amazon isn't responsible for any loss that occurs to items after the items are delivered to a third-party business and cannot issue refunds or replace these items.



For more information about freight forwarding, go to About Freight Forwarders and Hand Carry. For all other third-party companies:



* If an item arrives from Amazon with damaged packaging, please instruct the third-party company to refuse delivery. We'll issue a refund when the item is returned to Amazon.
* If an item arrives defective or damaged inside the packaging, go to Your Orders to view return options.
* If you received your package, but the item was missing from the box, go to Find a Missing Item from Your Order.
* If you have a missing package that shows as delivered, check with your third-party company. Follow the steps in Find a Missing Package that Shows as Delivered.



Amazon will not engage with a third-party company that did not make a purchase directly from Amazon.



Contact Amazon Customer Service if:



* The carrier delivered the item to the wrong address.
* You ordered a non-returnable item that was shipped in multiple boxes, and you only received a partial shipment.

# Find a Missing Item from Your Order



If an item is missing from your package, it may have shipped separately. Check Your Orders.



##### Find a missing item shipped by Amazon

If you received your shipping package and an entire item shipped by Amazon is missing, do the following:



1. Go to Your Orders and check if the missing item is in another shipment.
2. Select Track Package to find the delivery date for the shipment.
3. If there is no tracking information for the missing item, Customer Service will assist you. Otherwise, you can follow the recommendations in this page.



##### Find a missing item shipped and sold by a third-party seller



If the item missing from your package is shipped and sold by a third-party seller, contact the seller for assistance. Go to Contact a Third-Party Seller.



Learn how to identify orders shipped and sold by a seller by visiting Ordering from a third-party seller.



##### Part of an item is missing



Double check to confirm that the part is missing from the product packaging. If you can’t find it, contact the product's manufacturer for assistance.



Manufacturer contact information can be found on the product packaging, in the paperwork included with the product, or by searching the Internet.



If you can't find contact information, or if they can't help, you can return the item. Go to Your Orders and select Return or Replace Items.



##### Entire shipping package is missing



1. If the tracking info shows as delivered but you can't find the package, go to Find a Missing Package That Shows as Delivered for more information.
2. If the tracking info shows as undelivered, go to Undeliverable Packages.

#####

Check your delivery instructions in Your Addresses. The package could have been left where you requested.



##### 4 - Ask your household members and neighbors

Packages may be scanned as delivered before arrival.



##### 6 - You can check with the carrier



The carrier may have more information about the location of the package. To track your package on the carrier website or contact them, go to Carrier Contact Information.

If your order is shipped and sold by a third-party seller, contact the seller directly for assistance. Go to Contact a Third-Party Seller.

# Late Deliveries



What to do if your order didn't arrive on the estimated delivery date.



On this page:



* What to do if your delivery is late
* Why some deliveries are delayed
* How we’ll contact you if a delivery is late
* Why wait for 48 hours
* If 48 hours have passed since your estimated delivery date
* Guaranteed deliveries



##### What to do if your delivery is late



If your Amazon delivery is late:



* Track the package for status updates.
* Check your email for carrier notifications.
* Allow 48 hours past the estimated delivery date for unexpected delays.



##### Why some deliveries are delayed



Common reasons for late deliveries include:



* Bad weather
* Heavy traffic
* High delivery demand during holidays
* Custom delays for international packages
* Operational problems like a vehicle breaking down



##### How we’ll contact you if a delivery is late



If Amazon delivers the order, we’ll update your tracking with real-time information about where your package is. We’ll email you when we know there’s a delay. You’ll also find all correspondence in the Message Center.



For orders delivered by a delivery carrier, we’ll still update your Amazon order tracker. Your carrier will have the latest information. Contact your carrier directly for shipping updates. You can also check their website, emails, or mobile app for tracking information.



##### Why wait for 48 hours



Most deliveries arrive within 48 hours.

##### Orders Sold and Fulfilled by Amazon



A tracking number may not be on your order yet because it could still be on the way to the carrier. When the carrier scans your order the first time, you will have tracking details in Your Orders.



If no tracking is available and you need to return or exchange your order, wait for the estimated delivery date to pass. You'll be able to return or replace your order by visiting the Online Returns Center.



##### Orders Sold and Fulfilled by Third-Party Sellers



Seller orders can also be tracked, but only if the seller has shared this information with Amazon and you've chosen a traceable shipping method.



If a Track package option is not available, contact the seller as follows:



1. Go to Your Orders.
2. Find your order in the list.
3. Select Problem with Order.
4. Choose your topic from list displayed.
5. Select Contact Seller.



Allow two days for the seller to respond. In most cases you'll receive a reply sooner.



##### Tracking Your Return Order



Your return order status can be tracked by selecting View Return/Refund Status next to the relevant order. Go to Return Items You Ordered for more information.



##### Reasons Why Your Tracking Information Is Not Available



* Your package has not been scanned by the carrier. Issues like this can happen when shipping volume is high and packages are processed in bulk. Until this first scan, the carrier may not acknowledge receipt of the package.

# Push Notifications for Shipment Tracking



You can set up or edit push notifications to track your orders.



To receive push notifications for shipment tracking, ensure your phone settings allow app notifications and then turn on push notifications in the Amazon app.



##### Enable Push Notifications on Your Device



For iOS:



* Ensure you have the Amazon app (Go to the App Store and search Amazon)
* Go to Settings > Notifications > Amazon > Allow Notifications
* Turn on “Time Sensitive Notifications” and select all alert options for best notification of delivery



For Android:



* Ensure you have the Amazon app (Go to the App Store and search Amazon)
* Go to Settings > Notifications > App Notifications > Amazon > Turn on
* Turn on “Time Sensitive Notifications” and select all alert options for best notification of delivery



##### Check Your Push Notifications in the Amazon App



Go to the Amazon app > Your Account (Person icon in the bottom left corner of the app) > Settings (cog icon in the upper right hand corner of the app) > Notifications > Account and shipping. Turn on all for Push notifications.

# Tracking your package



You can find tracking numbers and delivery updates in Your Orders.



To get tracking numbers and delivery updates:



* Check Your Orders
* Find the order that you want to track
* Select Track package next to your order
* Select All updates to get:

  * delivery updates
  * tracking numbers
  * 

If you ordered more than one thing, they may have separate delivery dates and tracking info. Some packages, like standard international deliveries, might not be trackable.



Track packages from third-party sellers:



Third-party sellers at Amazon are independent businesses or individuals that sell their products on Amazon Marketplace. To check if you ordered from a third-party seller, refer to Your Orders and review the seller name.



You can only access tracking information if:



* the third-party seller shares this info with us
* you chose a trackable shipping option



To get help, refer to Contacting a third-party seller.



Understanding delivery statuses:



Your delivery status is in Your Orders:



* Arriving: usually followed by a date. Your package is on its way to you
* Out for delivery: your package will be delivered today
* Delivered: your package has been delivered, and might be in your safe space
* Undeliverable: your package couldn't be delivered, and will be returned to Amazon. When we receive it, we'll automatically issue you a refund. Visit the Undeliverable packages help page.

Fix a delivery issue:



Most issues can be fixed quickly. You can check what to do when:



* Order hasn't shipped yet: if you're expecting the package today and there isn't a status update, that's normal. Depending on the carrier, items may ship the same day they're delivered.
* Order is delayed: things like weather and traffic can cause delivery delays. Most late parcels arrive within 48 hours of the estimated delivery date. If the order has already been shipped, you can't cancel it.
* Missing package: if your package shows as delivered but you haven't received it, check the Find a missing package that shows as delivered page.
* Missing items: if you received a package that's missing an item, it may have been shipped separately. Check your orders, then refer to Find a missing item.
* To contact the company that's delivering your package, refer to Contact a carrier.

# Undeliverable Packages



This happens when an unplanned service disruption prevents us from delivering your package. We’ll notify you and refund you for the items you didn’t receive.



##### What Happens with Undelivered Packages



We automatically notify you. The undeliverable package is returned to Amazon, and we’ll process your refund.



When a package is returned as undeliverable, we can't reship it. You can place a new order for the item(s).



To stay on top of delivery changes, consider signing up to receive shipment updates via text message.



##### Refund Times for Undeliverable Packages



You can expect a refund within 7 business days after the return is received. Some issuing banks may require an additional 3-5 business days to process a refund.



Visit the Refunds Help page for more information about refunds processing times.



Undeliverable Orders Shipped and Sold by Sellers

If your order is shipped and sold by a third-party seller, contact the seller directly for assistance.



If you’re not satisfied with the seller solution or you don’t hear back from them within two business days, you may be eligible for a refund through Amazon's A-to-z Guarantee.



For more information about third-party sellers, visit Ordering from a Third-Party Seller.



##### Reasons for Undeliverable Packages



An undeliverable status occurs for various reasons including, but not limited:



* The shipment sustains damage before or during transit.

Visit Your Addresses to update your shipping addresses and add any delivery preferences. Customize instructions to include access codes, presence of dog, and preferred days of delivery for future orders.
* The shipping label becomes unreadable during transit.
* The carrier makes three unsuccessful attempts to access your delivery address.
* Customer/recipient refuses to accept package from carrier.
